title: Form 14A — Affidavit (General)
subtitle: Ontario Family Court — Family Law Rules (FLR 14A)
helpIntro: An Affidavit is a sworn statement of facts that supports your motion. You write out the facts — the who, what, when, and where — and then sign it in front of a commissioner of oaths (available at the courthouse or at most lawyers' offices, often for free). This is the document where you tell your story to the judge.

parts.title: Court information
parts.subtitle: Step 1 of 7
parts.intro: This pulls from your existing case file. Confirm the court details.

fields.label: Courthouse name
options: St. Catharines — Superior Court of Justice
Thunder Bay — Superior Court of Justice

fields.label: Court file number
Date of this affidavit
fields.helpText: The date you will sign this in front of the commissioner of oaths.

parts.title: The parties
parts.subtitle: Step 2 of 7
parts.intro: Confirm both parties' information. This auto-fills from your profile.

fields.label: Applicant's full legal name
Applicant's address for service
City
Postal code
Phone number
Respondent's full legal name
Respondent's address (if known)

parts.title: About you (the deponent)
parts.subtitle: Step 3 of 7
parts.intro: The 'deponent' is the person swearing or affirming this affidavit — that's you. These details go at the top of the affidavit.

fields.label: Your full legal name
fields.helpText: As it will appear on the sworn affidavit.

fields.label: Municipality and province where you live
fields.helpText: The form reads: 'I live in [municipality & province]'.

fields.label: Will you swear (religious oath) or affirm (non-religious)?
fields.helpText: Both are legally equal. Choose whichever you are comfortable with.

parts.title: Background facts
parts.subtitle: Step 4 of 7
parts.intro: Provide the factual background of your situation. Write clearly and stick to facts — not opinions or emotions. Each paragraph should cover one fact.

fields.label: Describe your relationship with the other party
fields.placeholder: e.g. I was married to the Respondent Jane Doe on June 15, 2012. We lived together at 123 Main Street, Toronto, Ontario. We separated on March 1, 2024.
fields.helpText: Include: how you are related, when the relationship started, when and how it ended.

fields.label: Describe the children involved (if any)
fields.placeholder: e.g. We have one child together: Alex Lance, born April 12, 2015. Alex currently lives with me at 123 Main Street, Toronto.
fields.helpText: Include: each child's name, date of birth, and where they are currently living. Leave blank if there are no children.

fields.label: What is happening right now? (current living/parenting arrangements)
fields.placeholder: e.g. Since our separation, Alex has been living with me. The Respondent sees Alex every other weekend. There is no formal court order in place.
fields.helpText: Describe the current situation — where everyone is living, what parenting time looks like, what support if any is being paid.

parts.title: Facts supporting your motion
parts.subtitle: Step 5 of 7
parts.intro: This is the most important part. Explain the facts that make your motion necessary. Be specific — include dates, names, and what happened. Write in numbered paragraphs.

fields.label: Why are you bringing this motion? What has happened that makes a court order necessary?
fields.placeholder:
e.g. 1. On May 1, 2026, the Respondent informed me that she intended to move with Alex to Calgary, Alberta without my consent.
2. I attempted to discuss this with the Respondent on May 5, 2026, but she refused to communicate.
3. I am concerned that if the Respondent moves, I will be denied parenting time with Alex.
fields.helpText: Number each paragraph. Each paragraph = one fact. Include who did what, when, and where. Do not include opinions — only facts you personally know or witnessed.

fields.label: What specific orders are you asking the court to make?
fields.placeholder:
e.g. 1. An order that the Applicant have temporary decision-making responsibility for Alex Lance.
2. An order that the Respondent not relocate with Alex outside of Toronto without court permission.
3. An order for child support of $X per month.
fields.helpText: These should match the orders listed on your Form 14. Be specific about what you want.

fields.label: Why are these orders in the best interests of the children? (if children are involved)
fields.placeholder: e.g. Alex is enrolled in school in Toronto and has a strong support network of family and friends here. Relocating to Calgary would disrupt Alex's education and my ongoing relationship with Alex.
fields.helpText: The court's primary concern is always what is best for the children. Explain why your requested orders serve the children's needs.

fields.label: Is this urgent? If yes, explain why it cannot wait.
fields.placeholder: e.g. The Respondent has stated she plans to move on July 1, 2026. Without an emergency order, the move will happen before this matter can be heard in the normal course.
fields.helpText: Only fill this in if you need the court to hear this motion on an urgent basis.

parts.title: Exhibits (attachments)
parts.subtitle: Step 6 of 7
parts.intro: List any documents you are attaching to this affidavit as exhibits. Label them Exhibit A, B, C, etc. in the order you mention them.

fields.label: Are you attaching any documents to this affidavit?
fields.helpText: Examples: text messages, emails, photos, prior court orders, letters.

fields.label: List your exhibits
fields.placeholder:
Exhibit A — Text messages between myself and the Respondent dated May 1–10, 2026
Exhibit B — Prior court order dated March 15, 2023
Exhibit C — Alex's school enrollment records
fields.helpText: For each exhibit: write the letter, then what it is and the date. Attach the actual document behind the affidavit when you file.

fields.label: Have you referenced each exhibit by letter in the facts section above?
fields.helpText: e.g. 'Attached as Exhibit A is a copy of the text messages.' The form requires you to identify each exhibit where it is first mentioned.

parts.title: Swearing / commissioning
parts.subtitle: Step 7 of 7
parts.intro: This section will be completed in front of a commissioner of oaths. You cannot sign this affidavit without a commissioner present — doing so makes it invalid.

fields.label: Where will you swear/affirm this affidavit?
fields.helpText: Commissioners of oaths are available free of charge at most Ontario courthouses.

fields.label: Municipality where it will be sworn
Province
Date to be commissioned (leave blank to fill at the courthouse)
I understand I must sign this affidavit in front of a commissioner of oaths for it to be valid. I confirm the facts I have written are true.
fields.helpText: Swearing a false affidavit is perjury — a criminal offence. Only include facts you know to be true.
